§ 01Enclosed: Letter of Intent

Dear {{recipientName}},

Following our work together through the qualification and diligence phases, we are pleased to enclose the Letter of Intent ("LOI") for the employee ownership transition of {{companyName}}.

This transmittal letter summarizes the key terms in plain language. We encourage you to review this summary first, then read the full LOI with your personal attorney and tax advisor before signing.

Important: The LOI is non-binding with respect to the transaction price and structure. It does, however, contain binding provisions regarding exclusivity, confidentiality, and the deposit. Please review those sections carefully.
§ 02Key Terms Summary
Transaction Terms
{{purchasePrice}}
ESOP trust acquires 100% of equity
{{entityConversion}}
{{sellerNoteTerms}}
{{sbaFinancing}}
{{exclusivityPeriod}}
Deposit & Retainer
$15,000 (already received — now uncashed and applied)
Credited toward Engagement Retainer upon LOI execution
{{exclusivityDeposit}}
§ 03What the LOI Triggers

Upon execution of the LOI, the following actions occur:

1.Deposit uncashed and applied. Your $15,000 pre-flight deposit is processed and credited toward the Engagement Retainer.
2.Exclusivity window opens. You agree not to solicit or negotiate with other buyers for the specified exclusivity period.
3.Diligence deepens. Forhemit initiates Gate 2 activities including the formal appraisal, QofE engagement, and SBA lender submission.
4.Team is fully engaged. Trustee, ERISA counsel, and appraiser begin substantive work on the transaction.
§ 04What to Discuss With Your Advisors

Before signing the LOI, we recommend discussing the following with your attorney and CPA:

A.§1042 Rollover Eligibility. Whether the transaction structure qualifies for tax deferral under IRC §1042, and the reinvestment requirements and timing.
B.Entity Conversion. If applicable, the implications of converting from LLC/S-Corp to C-Corp, including tax elections and timing.
C.Seller Note Terms. The interest rate, duration, and subordination terms of any seller-financed portion of the purchase price.
D.Post-Close Role. Your expected involvement in the company after closing, including employment terms and governance role.
§ 05Next Steps
1.Review the enclosed LOI with your personal attorney and CPA.
2.If you have questions or would like to discuss any term, contact Forhemit before signing.
3.Sign the LOI via the electronic signature link provided.
4.Upon execution, Forhemit will confirm receipt and initiate the next phase within 5 business days.
§ 06Contact

If you have questions about the LOI or any of its terms, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
{{ref}}

Please reference the above number in all correspondence related to this transaction.

—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
California Public Benefit Corporation
Authorized Representative

CONFIDENTIAL
This transmittal summarizes the LOI for convenience only. The full LOI governs in all cases.
